Serving All of McLennan County, TX

McLennan County Stucco Services

McLennan County homeowners know their exterior walls take a beating from Central Texas weather. From the established neighborhoods of Waco to the growing suburbs of Hewitt and Woodway, stucco remains a top choice for durable, attractive exteriors. Our team provides repair, restoration, and new installation services across the entire county, helping property owners protect their investment and improve curb appeal.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Reliable Stucco Work Across the Waco Area

The Waco area has experienced a surge of revitalization over the past several years, and with it has come increased demand for quality exterior work. Homeowners renovating older properties in East Waco and the Castle Heights area are choosing stucco for its clean, timeless look. Meanwhile, new subdivisions in Hewitt, Woodway, and China Spring feature stucco-clad homes that need proper maintenance to stay in top shape.

McLennan County's climate puts real demands on exterior surfaces. Summer temperatures regularly push past 100 degrees, and spring storm season brings hail, heavy rain, and high winds. Stucco walls absorb and release heat throughout these cycles, and over time the stress shows up as cracks, discoloration, and moisture-related problems.

We have spent years working in this area and understand the specific challenges that Waco-area homes face. Our repair and installation methods are tailored to perform under these conditions so your stucco lasts.

Stucco Services We Offer in McLennan County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout McLennan County.

Stucco Repair

From hairline cracks on a Hewitt home to large sections of failing stucco on a Waco bungalow, we handle repairs that range from cosmetic touch-ups to structural rebuilds. Every repair starts with a careful assessment of what is actually causing the problem.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

New construction in Woodway, Lorena, and the expanding western suburbs of Waco often calls for stucco exteriors. We install systems that are engineered for the local climate, with proper drainage planes and control joints to manage thermal movement.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

McLennan County's black clay soil is notorious for shifting, and that movement translates directly into cracks on stucco walls. We use flexible repair systems where appropriate and rigid fills where the substrate is stable to address each crack correctly.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Spring storms can drive rain behind stucco through small cracks and gaps that are easy to overlook. We have repaired water-damaged walls throughout the Waco area where moisture sat behind the finish for months, rotting sheathing and encouraging mold growth.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Many Waco homes built in the 1920s through the 1950s feature exterior plaster over masonry. We maintain and restore these finishes using materials and methods that are appropriate for the age of the structure, preserving the original character of the home.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

EIFS is common on commercial properties along the I-35 corridor through McLennan County. We repair impact damage, reseal joints, and replace failed sections to restore the insulated exterior and prevent further moisture problems.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Waco's growing downtown, the Baylor University area, and commercial districts in Hewitt and Woodway all have buildings with stucco exteriors. We work with business owners and general contractors to deliver clean, professional results on time.

Learn More

Local Expertise

We understand McLennan County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every McLennan County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all McLennan County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in McLennan County

The combination of intense heat, seasonal storms, and expansive clay soils makes McLennan County a demanding environment for stucco exteriors. These are the problems we encounter most frequently when inspecting homes and businesses across the Waco area.

Cracks

McLennan County's black clay soils swell when wet and shrink dramatically during dry spells. This constant foundation movement is the number one cause of stucco cracks on homes from Waco to Moody.

Bubbling or soft stucco

When the drainage plane behind stucco fails or was never installed properly, moisture becomes trapped against the wall. The result is soft spots and bubbling that indicate deeper problems beneath the surface.

Water stains

Mineral-rich well water from sprinkler overspray and rust runoff from aging metal components leave stubborn stains on stucco walls throughout the Waco suburbs. These marks worsen over time without proper cleaning and sealing.

Window and door leaks

Caulking and flashing around windows and doors deteriorate faster in McLennan County's heat. Once the seal breaks, wind-driven rain enters the wall cavity during spring and fall storms.

Failing patches

Homeowners who have attempted DIY stucco repairs or hired inexperienced contractors often end up with patches that crack, pop loose, or stand out visually against the original finish.

Separation around trim

The wide temperature range in the Waco area causes different materials to expand at different rates. Where stucco meets wood or metal trim, gaps develop over time and allow moisture behind the wall.

Impact damage

Hail is a regular occurrence during McLennan County's spring storm season. Even small hailstones can pit and chip stucco surfaces, and larger stones can punch through the finish coat entirely.

Faded or stained exterior walls

Months of direct sun exposure at temperatures above 100 degrees break down pigments in colored stucco. Walls that face south and west show the most fading, creating an uneven, weathered appearance across the home.

How We Work in McLennan County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule Inspection

Reach out by phone or through our website to arrange a visit. We inspect properties throughout McLennan County, from Waco and its suburbs to the smaller communities like Crawford and West.

2

Review Scope

We perform a hands-on inspection of your stucco, checking for cracks, soft spots, moisture indicators, and areas where the finish has pulled away. We explain what we find in plain language and answer your questions on the spot.

3

Get Estimate

After the inspection, you receive a written estimate that details the recommended work, materials, timeline, and cost. We stand behind our pricing and do not add charges once a project is underway.

4

Complete Work

Our crew arrives on schedule, completes the work to specification, and performs a final walkthrough with you. We make sure you are satisfied with the result before we consider the job done.
